data.title: GENIUS Act Proposal May Impact Stablecoin Issuers
data.description:
The proposed GENIUS Act could significantly affect stablecoin issuers by prohibiting them from offering interest or yields to holders. According to NS3.AI, this legislative move is expected to redirect capital towards yield-generating tokenized money market funds. Over the past six weeks, USDC has experienced net redemptions totaling approximately $6.5 billion, resulting in a reduction in stablecoin liquidity and diminishing short-term buying power in the crypto market.

Simultaneously, investors are reallocating funds from stablecoins to traditional safe-haven assets such as gold and silver. In response to these market shifts, Circle is increasingly concentrating on transaction activity and the adoption of real-world payments through strategic partnerships.

data.title: Bitcoin Transfer from Cumberland DRW to Anonymous Address
data.description: A transfer of 54.2 BTC was recorded at 14:46, moving from Cumberland DRW to an anonymous address, according to ChainCatcher. The transaction highlights ongoing activity in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: BitMart will be listed on USAT (USAT).
data.description: According to the official announcement, BitMart will list USAT on January 28th at 13:00 (UTC+8), and the USAT/USDT trading pair will be available. USAT is a federally regulated stablecoin issued by Tether in partnership with Anchorage Digital Bank N.A. and is applicable to GENIUS ACT.

data.title: Matrixport: Tightening stablecoin liquidity weakens short-term buying power in the crypto market.
data.description: Matrixport released a chart today stating that the GENIUS Act is expected to prohibit stablecoin providers from paying interest or returns to holders. Funds may therefore shift to yield-generating alternatives such as tokenized money market funds. USDC has seen net redemptions of approximately $6.5 billion over the past six weeks, leading to a contraction in stablecoin liquidity and weakening short-term purchasing power in the crypto market. Simultaneously, funds are flowing out of stablecoins and into traditional safe-haven assets such as gold and silver, further shrinking the stablecoin supply. Accordingly, Circle is shifting its focus from "market capitalization" to "trading activity" and is promoting the real-world use and payment of stablecoins through initiatives like the Circle Payment Network and partnerships with companies like Intuit.

data.title: Trader Utilizes High Leverage for SOL Investment
data.description:
A trader has opened a leveraged position on SOL, purchasing 10,000 units at an average price of $127.07, according to ChainCatcher. The transaction was executed at 14:21 with a leverage of 20 times. Currently, the position is experiencing a slight unrealized loss, while the account shows a total unrealized profit of $2.91 million.

The trader employs a high-leverage strategy, focusing primarily on mainstream cryptocurrencies and popular altcoins, indicating an aggressive trading approach.

data.title: Gemini's Europe CEO Calls for MiCA 2.0 to Enhance Crypto Regulation
data.description: One year after the implementation of the EU's MiCA regulations, Gemini's Europe CEO has underscored its importance as a groundbreaking framework for cryptocurrency regulation. According to NS3.AI, he pointed out several areas needing improvement, including ambiguous oversight and cumbersome rules. He advocates for a refined version, 'MiCA 2.0,' which would enhance the supervision structure, clarify jurisdictional boundaries, particularly concerning new crypto products, and establish stablecoin standards to prevent market instability. The article emphasizes the necessity for clear and balanced regulations, asserting that updates are crucial for Europe to sustain its leadership in the global cryptocurrency sector.

data.title: The Gate Index section will be the first to launch perpetual contract trading for BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) and EVIX (ETH Volatility Index).
data.description: According to the official announcement, the Gate Index Zone will launch live trading (USDT settlement) of BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) and EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) perpetual contracts on January 28th at 15:00 (UTC+8), supporting leverage from 1x to 50x. Gate Index perpetual contracts use mainstream global indices and volatility indices as underlying assets, introducing market sentiment indicators into the crypto derivatives trading system. While maintaining the advantages of USDT settlement and 24/7 trading, it provides users with trading options that are closer to the global macro market. As an important indicator for measuring market uncertainty, the rise and fall of VIX reflects changes in investors' expectations of risk and is often used for hedging and risk aversion. Its launch further provides investors with more professional trading tools, expanding the scope for cross-market trading and strategy deployment.

data.title: Binance Alpha will list Moonbirds (BIRB) on January 28th.
data.description: Binance Alpha will list Moonbirds (BIRB) on January 28th. Eligible users can claim the airdrop using Binance Alpha Points on the Alpha event page after trading opens on Alpha. Further details will be announced later.

data.title: OKX will launch silver (XAG) U-standard perpetual contracts, supporting up to 20x leverage.
data.description: OKX will officially launch XAGUSDT perpetual contracts on its website, app, and API at 20:00 Beijing time on January 28, 2026, supporting leverage up to 20x.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 900 USDT Mark with 24-Hour Increase
data.description: BNB has experienced a notable increase, surpassing the 900 USDT mark. According to Odaily, the current price of BNB is reported at 902 USDT, reflecting a 24-hour growth of 1.91%.

data.title: Daiwa Securities: Japan's fiscal expansion and weak yen may prompt the central bank to raise interest rates again.
data.description: Economists at Daiwa Securities suggest the Bank of Japan may raise policy rates again between April and June, potentially defying previous expectations. In a research report, economists noted, "Fiscal expansion and a weaker yen have exacerbated market concerns about rising prices, prompting calls for an earlier rate hike." They added that since Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda has hinted at closely monitoring price adjustments by Japanese companies in April, the central bank's branch governor meeting that month, along with April's CPI data for the Tokyo area, could be key factors in determining the next rate hike. (Jinshi)

data.title: BNB rises above 900 USDT
data.description: Market data shows that BNB has risen above 900 USDT and is currently trading at 902 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 1.91%.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Highlights Risks and Opportunities for Ethereum's Future
data.description: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has expressed concerns about the future of Ethereum, emphasizing risks beyond technical aspects. According to PANews, Buterin highlighted the potential danger of 'meaningless prosperity' at the application layer, where numerous applications may lack genuine social significance, reducing Ethereum's ecosystem to mere 'toys or casinos.' He urged developers to focus on three key areas: decentralized social networks (DeSoc), which aim to address user lock-in and migration freedom issues by creating truly user-owned and transferable social networks; smarter DAOs that go beyond simple token issuance and voting, designing governance structures that align with organizational goals and encourage diverse experimentation; and more decentralized stablecoins to enhance the financial infrastructure of the ecosystem.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank May Raise Policy Rate Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Economists from Daiwa Securities suggest that the Bank of Japan might increase its policy rate between April and June, contrary to previous expectations. According to Odaily, the economists' report highlights that fiscal expansion and a weakening yen have heightened market concerns about rising prices, prompting calls for an earlier rate hike. They further note that Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda has indicated he will closely monitor Japanese companies' price adjustments in April. The central bank's branch managers' meeting and Tokyo's April CPI data could be crucial in deciding the timing of the next rate increase.

data.title: XRP Faces Challenges in Breaking Trading Range
data.description: XRP has recently risen to approximately $1.90, yet it struggles to break free from a narrow trading range. According to NS3.AI, traders are paying close attention to the support level at $1.88 and the resistance zone between $1.94 and $2.00. The cryptocurrency continues to consolidate as it tests these significant price levels.

data.title: Institutional Outlook for the Fed's Interest Rate Decision: Rates Expected to Remain Unchanged
data.description: The following are institutional predictions for the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision, all of which believe the Fed will maintain the current rate: 1. Macquarie: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged; declining unemployment will guide the Fed to remain on hold. 2. Goldman Sachs: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged; the Fed will only make minor adjustments to its monetary policy stance. 3. Nomura: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged. Policy guidance will continue to indicate a higher threshold for future rate cuts. 4. Oxford Economics: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged. Economic growth will improve this year, and the Fed will maintain its current policy until June. 5. Citigroup: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged. The truly important signal will come from Powell's tone, especially the extent to which he opens the door to future rate cuts. 6. Barclays: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged; the combination of employment and inflation data does not support immediate further rate cuts, and the FOMC needs time to assess the impact of recent rate cuts. 7. Rabobank: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged. A majority of committee members believe that the three insurance rate cuts implemented at the end of last year were sufficient. 8. JPMorgan Chase: Expects the Fed to keep rates unchanged. The labor market has not deteriorated rapidly, and the recent stabilization of the unemployment rate should eventually lead the FOMC to reach a consensus to hold rates steady. 9. Morgan Stanley: Expects rates to remain unchanged. The statement is expected to revise its economic growth forecast from "moderate" to "solid" and remove the wording regarding "increased downside risks to employment." 10. KBC: Expects rates to remain unchanged. The downside risks to the labor market have eased, and real GDP growth is expected; these two factors together reduce the need for a rate cut at the January meeting. The Federal Reserve is scheduled to announce its interest rate decision at 3:00 AM on January 29th. (Jinshi)

data.title: The UK has banned Coinbase from publishing advertisements that downplay the risks of cryptocurrencies.
data.description: The UK Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) has banned a series of ads from Coinbase, arguing that they portrayed the cryptocurrency exchange as a solution to the cost of living while downplaying the risks of crypto investments. The ASA stated that the ads, including a satirical music video and three posters, were “irresponsible” and “downplayed the risks of cryptocurrencies.” They used humor to address serious financial problems and implied “change,” potentially misleading consumers into believing that complex, high-risk financial products were the simple or obvious solution. These ads had previously been rejected by Clearcast, the television advertising regulator, for similar reasons. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Thailand's Crackdown on Illegal Bitcoin Mining Operations
data.description: Thailand's special investigation force has uncovered a Bitcoin mining syndicate involving senior electricity officials accused of abusing their positions to conduct illegal mining operations. According to NS3.AI, thousands of mining rigs and substantial cash deposits were seized during raids conducted across multiple provinces as part of the ongoing 'Operation Copperhead'. This crackdown is part of Thailand's broader initiative to combat illicit cryptocurrency activities and aligns with similar global efforts to regulate unauthorized mining practices.

data.title: Japan's Financial Services Agency Seeks Public Input on Crypto Regulations
data.description:
Japan's Financial Services Agency has initiated a public consultation on draft implementation details concerning cryptocurrencies, electronic payment tools, and financial institutions. According to ChainCatcher, the draft outlines specific execution requirements following the 2025 revision of the Payment Services Act, including updates to official notices, administrative guidelines, and regulatory rules.

The draft covers various areas, such as the designation of new bonds as supporting assets, the regulatory framework for electronic payment tools and crypto-related intermediary services, and updated regulatory guidelines for financial institutions and their subsidiaries. The consultation will conclude on February 27, 2026, after which the regulations will take effect following the completion of necessary procedures, with results to be announced separately.

The Financial Services Agency is reportedly planning a comprehensive overhaul of the regulatory framework, aiming to introduce the country's first spot crypto ETFs by 2028. The roadmap includes reclassifying cryptocurrencies as 'specified assets' under the Investment Trust Law, reducing the crypto capital gains tax from a maximum of 55% to a flat 20%, and allowing time to strengthen custody and investor protection standards.
